items.title: Uber adds hotels to its app in big travel swing
items.summary: Open Uber's app and you'll see options to hail a car, order some food, or go grocery shopping. And starting today, you'll see something new: hotels. items.title: Google TV is getting a dedicated row for YouTube Shorts
items.summary: Google is making it easier to jump into watching YouTube Shorts on its TV devices without having to open the dedicated YouTube app. The Google TV homepage will soon introduce a new "Short videos for you" row that features a feed of personalized videos. items.title: Uber partners with Expedia to launch a hotel booking integration in its app in the US, and plans to add Expedia's Vrbo brand later in 2026 (Natalie Lung/Bloomberg)
items.summary:
Natalie Lung / Bloomberg:
Uber partners with Expedia to launch a hotel booking integration in its app in the US, and plans to add Expedia's Vrbo brand later in 2026  —  Uber Technologies Inc. is adding hotel bookings in its flagship app, marking the ride-hail company's biggest push yet into the travel market as it seeks to create an everything platform.

items.title: Tumbler Ridge families sue OpenAI for not alerting police to the suspect’s ChatGPT activity
items.summary: Seven families of victims injured or killed in the Tumbler Ridge school shooting in Canada have filed lawsuits against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, accusing the company and its leadership of negligence after they failed to alert police to the suspected shooter's ChatGPT activity. The families allege OpenAI stayed silent after its systems flagged activity […]

items.title: ChatGPT downloads are slowing — and may cause problems for OpenAI’s IPO
items.summary: ChatGPT is struggling to keep up its once-explosive growth as users uninstall the app or opt for rival chatbots instead. According to data from market intelligence firm Sensor Tower, ChatGPT experienced a 132 percent increase in uninstalls year over year in April. Its uninstall rate was even higher last month, up 413 percent year-over-year, following […]

items.title: EGO’s carbon fiber POWERLOAD 16-inch string trimmer with LINE IQ + 2x batteries can be yours at $330 ($228 off)
items.summary:
Over at Amazon, you can still pick up EGO’s advanced 56V 16-inch Telescopic Carbon Fiber POWERLOAD LINE IQ Cordless Electric String Trimmer with 2x batteries for $329.99 shipped. This $558 bundle package kept down around $459 until April began, which is when we first saw it fall lower to this same rate, which is where it’s been holding strong. Seeing as buying the typical string trimmer and 4.0Ah battery package with the extra 2.5Ah battery separate at their current discounted rates will run you $394 in total right now ($294 for the trimmer kit + $100 for the extra battery), and even more if they were at full price, that makes jumping on this deal all the better. Doing so gives you a 41% markdown off the going rate for $228 in savings, letting you add it to your arsenal while keeping to its second-lowest tracked price – the best deal we’ve seen since July 2025 Prime Day.

If you’d rather not pay full price, Best Buy is now offering an open-box “Excellent” Xbox Ally X for $869.99 shipped. That’s $130 off its typical going rate and the lowest price we’ve tracked so far. We’ve previously seen open-box units hover closer to the $900 range, but this listing dips further to the $870 mark. You can save even more with an open-box “Fair” unit, currently down to $809.99 shipped, delivering up to $190 in savings. There’s also an open-box “Excellent” standard Xbox Ally available for $526.99 shipped, which undercuts the current sale price of a brand-new unit by $73. These are some of the best prices we’ve seen yet on either handheld, so it’s worth taking a closer look below.
